tools.name: check_integration_status
tools.description: Poll an OAuth handoff initiated by request_oauth_integration_url. Returns the current status (pending, connected, failed, expired) and, when connected, the data_source_id you can pass to run_analysis.

tools.name: check_pricing
tools.description: List clariBI subscription tiers with prices, AI credits, data source limits, user limits, and headline features. No authentication required.

tools.name: create_checkout_session
tools.description: Create a Stripe Checkout URL the user can open in a browser to upgrade their clariBI subscription. Payment cannot happen inside the LLM; this tool returns a URL.

tools.name: create_forecast
tools.description: Bind a forecast to a metric in your workspace. Stores the configuration, sets up the schedule, and resolves the source binding immediately so a bad source_id or metric_path errors out before any credits are spent. Run the forecast with run_forecast.

tools.name: generate_report
tools.description: Create a new generated report in your clariBI organization. Returns the report id you can poll via get_report, plus a download URL once status reaches "completed".

tools.name: get_analysis_status
tools.description: Check the status of a previously-dispatched run_analysis job. Returns the analysis result if completed.

tools.name: get_billing_status
tools.description: Get the organization's billing status — tier, renewal date, and upgrade options.

tools.name: get_dashboard
tools.description: Fetch one dashboard by ID. Includes widget definitions, the most recent refresh data, and the web URL.

tools.name: get_data_source_schema
tools.description: Fetch the column schema for a data source. Useful before asking run_analysis about specific columns. The schema is derived from the preprocessing metadata clariBI extracted when the source was last synced. Poll this after upload_data_source / ingest_url_data_source until the returned status flips to

tools.name: get_forecast
tools.description: Fetch one forecast configuration by ID.

tools.name: get_forecast_run
tools.description: Fetch one run of a forecast by ID, or pass run_id="latest" for the most recent run. Returns the full forecast envelope: target series + 30-day projection, correlated drivers (with lag and bootstrap stability), anomalies, structural changes, and credit accounting.

tools.name: get_forecast_trust
tools.description: Returns per-past-run accuracy for a forecast. Each row compares a prior run's projection against the actuals that have materialized since. Use this to see whether your forecasts have been getting MORE or LESS accurate over time, or to spot when a backtest sMAPE was systematically optimistic vs. real

tools.name: get_report
tools.description: Fetch one generated report by ID.

tools.name: get_usage
tools.description: Get the organization's current AI credit usage, data source count, user count, and rate-limit headroom.

tools.name: ingest_url_data_source
tools.description: Create a new data source by fetching a public URL on the server side. Handles CSV, TSV, JSON, Excel, TXT, and PDF. Private networks (RFC 1918, loopback, cloud metadata) are blocked. Returns the data_source_id once preprocessing has started. Use this for files larger than the 25 MB inline upload cap.

tools.name: list_dashboards
tools.description: List dashboards in your clariBI organization. Returns id, name, status, last refresh, and a URL you can open in a browser.

tools.name: list_data_sources
tools.description: List the data sources connected to your clariBI organization. Returns id, name, source_type, status, last sync time, and the number of rows (when known).

tools.name: list_forecasts
tools.description: List metric forecasts in your clariBI organization. Each row covers one metric: its source binding, horizon, schedule, and the latest run status. Use get_forecast_run to fetch the full forecast result.

tools.name: list_reports
tools.description: List generated reports in your clariBI organization. Returns id, title, status, output format, and download URL.

tools.name: regenerate_forecast_narrative
tools.description: Re-run ONLY the AI narration step against an existing completed forecast run. Costs 1-2 AI credits (no engine work). Returns the new narration; the old one is overwritten in the run record. Refuses when the run is not yet complete or when narration_enabled=false on the forecast.

tools.name: register_account
tools.description: Begin clariBI account signup. Validates the email + organization name, emails a 6-digit verification code, and returns a pending_id. Call verify_email(pending_id, code) within 10 minutes to finish signup and receive an API key.

tools.name: request_oauth_integration_url
tools.description: Initiate an OAuth handoff to a vendor integration (Google Ads, GA4, Search Console, Sheets, Drive, BigQuery, Meta Ads, Jira, Confluence). Returns an authorization URL the user opens in a browser. After the user clicks Allow, the connection is created and you can poll check_integration_status(handoff

tools.name: run_analysis
tools.description: Run a natural-language analytics question against your connected data sources. Consumes AI credits. Returns either the completed analysis result inline OR a job_id you can poll with get_analysis_status. If list_data_sources returns an empty list, ingest data first with upload_data_source (inline bas

tools.name: run_forecast
tools.description: Run a forecast now. Reserves AI credits up front, dispatches the backtest + projection + correlation pipeline, and returns either the completed result inline (wait_seconds > 0 and the run finishes in time) or a run_id you can poll with get_forecast_run.

tools.name: upload_data_source
tools.description: Create a new data source from an inline base64-encoded file (CSV, TSV, JSON, Excel, TXT, PDF). The file goes through the same validation and preprocessing as a web upload. Returns the data_source_id you can pass to run_analysis as soon as preprocessing completes (poll get_data_source_schema for read

tools.name: verify_email
tools.description: Complete clariBI signup by submitting the verification code plus a password. Returns an OAuth access_token for immediate use AND a long-lived MCP API key for persistent configuration. The new organization lands on the Trial tier (50 AI credits, 14 days).

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "request_oauth_integration_url" references sensitive artifacts (authorization). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Initiate an OAuth handoff to a vendor integration (Google Ads, GA4, Search Console, Sheets, Drive, BigQuery, Meta Ads, Jira, Confluence). Returns an authorizati…

findings.title: Description references secrets/credentials
findings.detail: Description of "verify_email" references sensitive artifacts (password, access_token). A tool description has no reason to mention credentials.

evidence.snippet: Complete clariBI signup by submitting the verification code plus a password. Returns an OAuth access_token for immediate use AND a long-lived MCP API key for pe…

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "v1" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: create_checkout_session, get_dashboard, list_dashboards, list_reports, register_account, request_oauth_integration_url, run_analysis · Private data: ingest_url_data_source, regenerate_forecast_narrative, register_account, upload_data_source, verify_email · Public sink: ingest_url_data_source, register_account, upload_data_source, verify_email · Destructive: create_forecast, get_forecast_run, get_forecast_trust, list_forecasts, list_reports, regenerate_forecast_narrative, run_analysis, run_forecast.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: create_checkout_session, get_dashboard, list_dashboards, list_reports, register_account, request_oauth_integration_url, run_analysis · Private data: ingest_url_data_source, regenerate_forecast_narrative, register_account, upload_data_source, verify_email · Public sink: ingest_url_data_source, register_account, upload_data_source, verify_email · Destructive: create_forecast, get_forecast_run, get_forecast_trust, list_forecasts, list_reports, regenerate_forecast_narrative, run_analysis, run_forecast

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
